name: Handle Reference
description: Add or remove the reference channel (zero-filled). When adding, coordinates are auto-resolved from EEG.urchanlocs first.

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: Action to perform. 'auto': remove if present, add if missing. 'add': add zero-filled channel. 'remove': remove the channel.

inputs.name: locfile
inputs.description: [Optional fallback] Channel location file (.ced, .sfp, .elp, .xyz, .loc). Priority: (1) EEG.urchanlocs is checked first automatically — safe, matching coordinate system. (2) This file is only used if not found there. (3) Label only if neither has the channel.

outputs.name: EEG
outputs.description: Output EEG structure with reference channel added or removed

help_text:
CUSTOM_HANDLEREF - Add or remove the reference channel from an EEG dataset

Usage:
  >> [EEG, action] = custom_handleref(EEG, 'key', 'val');

Inputs:
  EEG        - Input EEGLAB EEG structure

Optional inputs:
  'ref'      - [string] Label of the reference channel (e.g. 'Cz').
               {default: 'Cz'}
  'action'   - ['auto'|'add'|'remove'] Action to perform.
               'auto'   - If the channel exists, remove it; if missing,
                          add a zero-filled channel at the end. (default)
               'add'    - Add a zero-filled reference channel. Errors if
                          the channel already exists.
               'remove' - Remove the reference channel. Errors if the
                          channel is not found.
  'locfile'  - [string] Path to a channel location file (.ced, .sfp,
               .elp, .xyz, .loc). This is a FALLBACK only. See coordinate
               lookup priority below. {default: ''}

Outputs:
  EEG        - Output EEGLAB EEG structure
  action     - [string] Action that was performed: 'added' or 'removed'

Coordinate lookup priority (when adding):
  1. EEG.urchanlocs — Original channel locations preserved by EEGLAB.
     These are guaranteed to match the dataset's coordinate system
     (nose direction, units, etc.). This is checked AUTOMATICALLY.
  2. locfile — External channel location file. Used only if the channel
     is NOT found in EEG.urchanlocs. WARNING: nose direction and
     coordinate conventions may differ from the dataset.
  3. Label only — If neither source has the channel, it is added with
     the label only (no coordinates).

Notes:
  When adding a reference channel, a row of zeros is appended to the
  data matrix. This is appropriate for data that has already been
  re-referenced (e.g. average reference), where the original reference
  electrode carries a flat signal by definition.

Author: Yong Oh, 2025
